chunk_summary: This section emphasizes maternal-fetal risk assessment and program linkages during stunting visits, focusing on intergenerational risks, maternal anemia, and nutritional interventions to prevent chronic undernutrition.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of maternal-fetal risk factors in early growth faltering and stunting.
Learn how to integrate maternal anemia and nutritional supplementation programs into stunting management.
Identify key determinants like postpartum depression and caregiver stress that impact child nutrition.
Explore preventive strategies for adolescent girls to reduce intergenerational stunting risks.
key_facts: Maternal anemia and untreated health issues directly reduce feeding capacity and adherence to follow-ups.
Small-for-gestational-age status and poor early weight gain predict later stunting risk.
National programs like AMB and WIFS provide iron-folic acid supplementation to mothers and adolescent girls.
Calcium supplementation during pregnancy and lactation is recommended as per national guidelines.
Dietary diversity should align with ICMR-NIN food-group targets for optimal nutrition.
reference_quote: Ensure mothers and adolescent girls in the household are connected to iron–folic acid supplementation platforms (including AMB and WIFS where applicable).
document_key_topics: Program linkages for anemia and nutrition
